insight.title: Why could you switch tabs and send a stamp?
insight.subtitle: Badges and icons guide your actions
insight.badgeDesc: A red number badge means unread messages. This is an international symbol shared across SNS email and messaging apps that naturally guides users.
insight.stampTitle: Emoji icon equals gateway to stamps
insight.stampDesc: The smiley icon means open emoji stamp panel a globally universal UI. Even without reading language this icons meaning is understood worldwide.
insight.tabDesc: Bottom navigation tabs list what you can do in this app. Icon plus number badge naturally guides you to your destination.
insight.inputTitle: Input field is a universal UI
insight.inputDesc: The text input box plus send button combination is adopted in chat apps worldwide. Once experienced you can intuitively operate it regardless of language.
insight.sendDesc: Right-pointing arrow and paper plane icons have become established across cultures as symbols meaning send or proceed.
insight.bubbleDesc: Right is me Left is other. This bubble layout is the de facto standard of chat UI.

quiz2.missionText: Please react to the message with a heart

insight.title: How did you find the reaction button?
insight.subtitle: The small icon beside the bubble teaches the action
insight.reactionDesc: Reaction emoji are established worldwide across SNS and chat apps as a way to convey emotion without a reply.
insight.iconTitle: The emoji icon beside the speech bubble
insight.iconDesc: The emoji icon shown next to received messages indicates you can react to this message. Even small its position and shape carry meaning.
insight.heartTitle: Heart is the universal language of like
insight.heartDesc: Across all platforms the heart means positive reaction the most widely used emoji symbol regardless of platform.
insight.emojiDesc: Emoji convey emotions directly without relying on language.
insight.colorDesc: The green color is deeply associated with messaging apps.

insight.title: How did you know the plus button could send images?
insight.subtitle: Media sending is handled by the add icon
insight.plusTitle: Plus sign means something can be added
insight.plusDesc: The plus icon on the left of the input field shows you can send more than text. Camera files location various media entry points are hidden here a universal UI pattern.
insight.photoTitle: Camera icon is universal for image
insight.photoDesc: The camera icon does not depend on language. It is established in all cultures as a symbol for photo image and you can predict tapping it opens image selection.
insight.galleryDesc: The bottom sheet sliding up from below shows candidates you can select with this action. When an image grid appears selecting and sending becomes intuitively clear.
insight.longPressDesc: UIs that do not respond to a tap often show additional menus on long press.
insight.contextMenuDesc: The menu appearing on long press lists what you can do with this item.
insight.destructiveDesc: Delete buttons are red to warn about irreversible actions.

insight.title: How did you know long press could unsend?
insight.subtitle: Multiple layers of UI literacy combine to solve it
insight.listDesc: The chat list is ordered by most recently messaged. The top being the most recent talk room is a rule shared across all messaging apps.
insight.longPressDesc: UIs that do not respond to a tap often show additional menus on long press. Established as a standard interaction users unconsciously try it.
insight.redTitle: Red text warns of dangerous operation
insight.redDesc: Irreversible actions like unsend and delete are displayed in red by UI design convention. Red equals danger ingrained from traffic lights as a cultural symbol.
insight.plusDesc: The plus icon is a universal symbol meaning add or create.
insight.wizardTitle: Step format breaks down complex operations
insight.wizardDesc: The flow breaks complex actions into small steps teaching what to do next.
insight.checkboxDesc: Checkboxes with check marks intuitively indicate selection state.
